name: linear-issues-write
description: Create and update Linear issues via CLI (write operations)
capabilities: Create new issues with title, description, team, assignee, priority, labels, and project
Update existing issues including title, description, status, assignee, priority, and labels
Resolve team, user, and label names automatically (case-insensitive)
Output results as JSON for scripting integration
Add labels without removing existing labels
Remove project or assignee from issues
author: Bind
securitySummary: This skill is a legitimate CLI wrapper for Linear API operations. The static analysis detected 64 findings, but all are FALSE POSITIVES: version strings misidentified as weak crypto (e.g., '^29.0.0'), documentation examples flagged as shell execution, and legitimate API key references marked as secret access. The skill uses the official @linear/sdk package, requires standard LINEAR_API_KEY authentication, and performs only documented create/update operations. No malicious intent or data exfiltration patterns found.
valueStatement: Creating and updating Linear issues requires navigating the web interface. This skill provides command-line tools to create issues, set priorities, assign team members, and update issue status directly from your terminal.
userTitle: Create and update Linear issues from CLI
limitations: Requires bun runtime to be installed
Only supports write operations (create and update), not read or delete
Team, user, and label names must exist in your Linear workspace

useCases.title: Quick issue creation
useCases.description: Create bugs and feature requests from terminal without switching to browser

useCases.title: Status workflow updates
useCases.description: Update issue status and assignees during daily standups or after completing tasks

useCases.title: Automated issue tracking
useCases.description: Script automated issue creation from CI/CD pipelines or monitoring alerts

promptTemplates.title: Create simple issue
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Linear issue with title 'Fix login timeout bug', team 'Engineering', priority 'high', and description 'Users cannot login after 5 minutes of inactivity'

promptTemplates.title: Create detailed issue
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Linear issue titled 'Add dark mode support', assigned to 'Sarah Chen', with labels 'Feature' and 'UI', priority 'medium', and project 'Main App'

promptTemplates.title: Update issue status
promptTemplates.prompt: Update issue ENG-456 to status 'In Progress' and assign it to 'Michael Torres'

promptTemplates.title: Bulk update labels
promptTemplates.prompt: Update issue PROJ-789 by adding labels 'Q1' and 'Security' without removing existing labels

bestPractices: Store your LINEAR_API_KEY securely in environment variables, not in scripts
Use the --json flag when integrating with other tools or automation scripts
Verify team and user names exist before creating issues to avoid errors
antiPatterns: Hardcoding API keys directly in command arguments or scripts
Creating issues without descriptions when details are important for context
Using the same issue for multiple unrelated changes or requests

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Check individual tool documentation for compatibility.

faq.question: What are the rate limits?
faq.answer: Rate limits are determined by your Linear subscription plan. The skill uses the official Linear SDK which respects these limits.

faq.question: Can I use this with other issue trackers?
faq.answer: No. This skill is specifically designed for Linear. Use separate skills for Jira, GitHub Issues, or other platforms.

faq.question: Is my data safe?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill uses your LINEAR_API_KEY for authentication. Data is transmitted directly between your environment and Linear servers.

faq.question: Why am I getting 'team not found' errors?
faq.answer: Verify the team name matches exactly (case-insensitive) with your Linear workspace. Team names must exist and be accessible with your API key.

faq.question: How is this different from linear-issues-read?
faq.answer: This skill (linear-issues-write) creates and updates issues. The linear-issues-read skill lists and searches existing issues. Use both together for full workflow support.
